verses.text: Levi's line comes quickly to its center: through Kohath and Amram to Aaron, Moses, and Miriam, and Aaron's sons. The priestly tribe is set at the heart of the genealogy. God gave His people a tribe to mediate and to lead in worship.

Levi's line comes quickly to its center: through Kohath and Amram to Aaron, Moses, and Miriam, and Aaron's sons. The priestly tribe is set at the heart of the genealogy. God gave His people a tribe to mediate and to lead in worship.

Levi's line comes quickly to its center: through Kohath and Amram to Aaron, Moses, and Miriam, and Aaron's sons. The priestly tribe is set at the heart of the genealogy. God gave His people a tribe to mediate and to lead in worship.

The high-priestly succession runs from Eleazar and Phinehas down through the generations to Zadok. An unbroken line of priests served between God and Israel. The LORD provided continual mediation for His people, age after age.

The high-priestly succession runs from Eleazar and Phinehas down through the generations to Zadok. An unbroken line of priests served between God and Israel. The LORD provided continual mediation for His people, age after age.

The high-priestly succession runs from Eleazar and Phinehas down through the generations to Zadok. An unbroken line of priests served between God and Israel. The LORD provided continual mediation for His people, age after age.

The priestly line continues, including the Azariah who served in Solomon's temple. The succession is tied to the house God chose to fill. Each generation had its priest to keep the worship of the LORD alive.

The priestly line continues, including the Azariah who served in Solomon's temple. The succession is tied to the house God chose to fill. Each generation had its priest to keep the worship of the LORD alive.

The priestly line continues, including the Azariah who served in Solomon's temple. The succession is tied to the house God chose to fill. Each generation had its priest to keep the worship of the LORD alive.

The line runs down to Jehozadak, who 'went into captivity when the LORD carried away Judah.' Even the priesthood went into exile. The sacred office was not exempt from judgment, yet, as the return showed, it was not extinguished.

The line runs down to Jehozadak, who 'went into captivity when the LORD carried away Judah.' Even the priesthood went into exile. The sacred office was not exempt from judgment, yet, as the return showed, it was not extinguished.

The three great Levite families — Gershom, Kohath, Merari — are named, the divisions among whom all temple service was shared. God ordered His worship through appointed families. The work of His house was distributed, not left to chance.

The three great Levite families — Gershom, Kohath, Merari — are named, the divisions among whom all temple service was shared. God ordered His worship through appointed families. The work of His house was distributed, not left to chance.

The three great Levite families — Gershom, Kohath, Merari — are named, the divisions among whom all temple service was shared. God ordered His worship through appointed families. The work of His house was distributed, not left to chance.
Gershom's line is traced through its generations. The branch that bore no priests still served the LORD's house. Every Levite family had its part; no role in God's service was without honour.

Kohath's line runs down through Elkanah and Jeroham. Buried in these names is the family of Samuel the prophet. God raised His great servants from within the tribe set apart for Him.
Kohath's line runs down through Elkanah and Jeroham. Buried in these names is the family of Samuel the prophet. God raised His great servants from within the tribe set apart for Him.

Kohath's line runs down through Elkanah and Jeroham. Buried in these names is the family of Samuel the prophet. God raised His great servants from within the tribe set apart for Him.

The sons of Samuel and the line of Merari are recorded. Even the prophet's own household takes its place among the Levites. Greatness in God's service did not exempt a family from the ordinary ranks of duty.

These are the singers David set over the song of the LORD's house once the ark had rest, with Heman's line traced back to Israel. God appointed a ministry of music. Sung praise was no afterthought but a settled, honoured calling.

These are the singers David set over the song of the LORD's house once the ark had rest, with Heman's line traced back to Israel. God appointed a ministry of music. Sung praise was no afterthought but a settled, honoured calling.

Heman's descent is followed up through Kohath to Levi and Israel. The chief singer's lineage is rooted in the covenant people. Those who led God's praise were grounded deep in the family of faith.
Heman's descent is followed up through Kohath to Levi and Israel. The chief singer's lineage is rooted in the covenant people. Those who led God's praise were grounded deep in the family of faith.

Asaph, who stood at Heman's right hand, has his line traced through Gershom. The psalmist whose name heads many psalms is honoured here. God remembers those who gave Him their voices and their songs.

Ethan of Merari and his line stood on the left, and the rest of the Levites were appointed to all the service of the tabernacle. Every family had its station. God's worship ran on the ordered ministry of many hands.

Aaron and his sons alone offered on the altar and made atonement, as Moses commanded; the priestly line is named again. The work of atonement was reserved to the priesthood. Sinners could draw near to God only through appointed sacrifice.

Aaron and his sons alone offered on the altar and made atonement, as Moses commanded; the priestly line is named again. The work of atonement was reserved to the priesthood. Sinners could draw near to God only through appointed sacrifice.

The dwelling places of Aaron's sons begin with Hebron, given them by lot, though its fields went to Caleb. The priests were settled among the tribes. God planted those who served Him throughout the land, near to all His people.

The dwelling places of Aaron's sons begin with Hebron, given them by lot, though its fields went to Caleb. The priests were settled among the tribes. God planted those who served Him throughout the land, near to all His people.

The cities of refuge and other towns given to the priests are listed. Among the priestly cities were places where the manslayer could flee for safety. Justice and mercy met in the towns of God's ministers.

Cities are assigned by lot to the remaining Kohathites, Gershomites, and Merarites. The Levites received no single territory but towns scattered everywhere. Their inheritance was the LORD, and their presence reached every tribe.

Cities are assigned by lot to the remaining Kohathites, Gershomites, and Merarites. The Levites received no single territory but towns scattered everywhere. Their inheritance was the LORD, and their presence reached every tribe.

Israel gives the Levites cities with their pasture lands throughout the tribes. The whole nation provided for those who served God's house. Worship and the teaching of the law were thus woven through all the land.

Israel gives the Levites cities with their pasture lands throughout the tribes. The whole nation provided for those who served God's house. Worship and the teaching of the law were thus woven through all the land.

Israel gives the Levites cities with their pasture lands throughout the tribes. The whole nation provided for those who served God's house. Worship and the teaching of the law were thus woven through all the land.

More cities of refuge and Levitical towns are named across Ephraim, Manasseh, and beyond. The priestly tribe was deliberately dispersed. God set His servants among every people so none would be without the knowledge of Him.

The Levitical cities in Issachar, Asher, and Naphtali are listed. Even the far northern tribes had Levites in their midst. No corner of Israel was left without those set apart to serve and teach God's word.

The cities of Merari in Zebulun, Reuben, and Gad close the list. The whole tribe of Levi, landless by design, was scattered as salt through Israel. Their portion was God Himself, their calling to fill the land with His worship.
